conflict: At Saint's Basin and under Walter Knapp's pressure, August is cornered by memories of his father, his own choices, and the knowledge that procedure will not save his name anyway.
consequence: August offers a compromised confession by surrendering the true ledger page and waiving Cora's deficiency after foreclosure, but he still permits the auction, preserving his moral ambiguity.
must_land_beats: August's night visit to the basin feels like reckoning, not redemption.
Walter Knapp reveals that Morrow's institutional death is already mostly decided.
The true ledger page is passed out of August's control.
August's concession helps Cora materially while refusing to erase the damage he helped do.
objective: Decide whether August will keep protecting the bank's version of events or yield any truth before Morrow is liquidated.
